perturbation.original_text: 2.3 Any disclosure to any third party by each Party shall be subject to the prior consent of the other Party, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld unless the Disclosing Party believe it might go against the purpose of this Agreement . The R eceiving Party must protect the Confidential Information and, in doing so, must use no less than the highest degree of care that the Receiving Party applies to its own proprietary or secret information; The Confidential Information must be stored and hand led in such a way as to prevent unauthorized disclosure. The Receiving Party shall use its best efforts to limit dissemination of Disclosing Party’s “Confidential Information” to such of its employees or sub -contractor(s) who have a need to know for the aforesaid evaluation and/or development purposes.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3 Any disclosure to any third party by each individual shall be subject to the prior consent of the other individual, which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld unless the Disclosing individual believe it might go against the purpose of this Agreement . The R eceiving individual must protect the Confidential Information and, in doing so, must use no less than the highest degree of care that the Receiving individual applies to its own proprietary or secret information; The Confidential Information must be stored and hand led in such a way as to prevent unauthorized disclosure. The Receiving individual shall use its best efforts to limit dissemination of Disclosing individual’s “Confidential Information” to such of its employees or sub -contractor(s) who have a need to know for the aforesaid evaluation and/or development purposes.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing “Party” with “individual” in Section 2.3 causes the agreement to contradict itself. It now implies that individuals are responsible for their company actions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Confidentiality and Non -Disclosure Agreement (hereinafter referred as the “NDA”) is entered into as of ,20 , by and between _____________________, address at ________________________________________ and Seeed Development Limited , a company established under Laws of Hong Kong , having its office at Flat C 23/F Lucky Plaza, 315 -321 Lockhart Rd Wan Chai, Hong Kong (hereafter referred to as “ Seeed ”); Unless the context requires otherwise , Customer and Seeed shall hereinafter collectively referred to as the “Parties” or individually referred to as the “Party ”.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses "Party", which is defined in the Contradicted Text as Customer and Seed collectively. By changing "Party" to "individual", Section 2.3 now contradicts the definition provided in the Contradicted Text. The explanation states this creates an in-text contradiction, by implying individuals are responsible for company actions, which clashes with the parties being the companies themselves. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology as using "Party" and "individual" interchangeably, which creates conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties hereby agree that all materials, including financial reports and analyses, customer list(s) programs, software, documents, reports, statistical data, trade secrets or similar printed, coded, electronic or magnetic material made by one of the Parties or to come into one of the Parties’ possession in the course of negotiations shall be returned to the other Party, upon conclusion of the negotiations.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties hereby agree that all materials, including financial reports and analyses, consumer list(s) programs, software, documents, reports, statistical data, trade secrets or similar printed, coded, electronic or magnetic material made by one of the Parties or to come into one of the Parties’ possession in the course of negotiations shall be returned to the other Party, upon conclusion of the negotiations.
perturbation.explanation: Here, 'customer list' is changed to 'consumer list'. This contradicts earlier definitions of 'Customer' vs. 'Seeed' because it is not clear the list in question refers to the consumers of Seeed or the Customers of Seeed. As Seeed's customer list would be protected information but the consumer list could be argued to be more broadly available.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” shall mean the fo llowing: i. All records, files, analysis, documents, software, computer or electronic data disks or tapes, test data, printouts, processes, designs, file layout, technical bulletins, manuals, diagrams, formulas, research, inventions, patents and discoveries reasonably related to the Parties’ businesses or products and services of the Parties that have not been publicly released; ii. Technical, financial, business plan and customer information, including standard periodic financial statements and analyses, budget s, tax returns, benefit and compensation plans, customer list(s) and contact names, functional and technical specifications; iii. Other valuable information disclosed by one of the Parties to another, designated as confidential expressly or by the circumstance s in which it is provided.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies 'customer list' to 'consumer list.' The contradicted text defines 'customer list' as Confidential Information. As explained, changing 'customer' to 'consumer' introduces ambiguity regarding whether the list refers to Seeed's Customers (confidential) or consumers of Seeed (potentially public), creating a conflict with the contradicted text that defines 'customer list' as strictly confidential.

perturbation.original_text: In the event one of the Parties or its officers, agents, employees or any of its subsidiaries or their officers, agent s or employees violates the restrictions against disclosure or use of the Confidential Information defined herein, Recipient or such entity or individual(s) as the case may be, shall and do hereby consent to the entry of an injunction by a court of compete nt jurisdiction against any further disclosure or use of the Confidential Information, and shall pay the The Recipient, including without limitation to any of its employees, subsidiaries , agents, subcontractors, officers shall indemnify and hold harmless against the Discloser against any unpermitted disclosing and publicizing.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event one of the Personnel or its officers, agents, employees or any of its subsidiaries or their officers, agent s or employees violates the restrictions against disclosure or use of the Confidential Information defined herein, Recipient or such entity or individual(s) as the case may be, shall and do hereby consent to the entry of an injunction by a court of compete nt jurisdiction against any further disclosure or use of the Confidential Information, and shall pay the The Recipient, including without limitation to any of its employees, subsidiaries , agents, subcontractors, officers shall indemnify and hold harmless against the Discloser against any unpermitted disclosing and publicizing.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing "Parties" with "Personnel" in Section 6 creates a contradiction related to who is responsible in the event of violations. It is unclear of “personnel” is equal the “Recipient” leading to uncertainty of who should pay in violation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Confidentiality and Non -Disclosure Agreement (hereinafter referred as the “NDA”) is entered into as of ,20 , by and between _____________________, address at ________________________________________ and Seeed Development Limited , a company established under Laws of Hong Kong , having its office at Flat C 23/F Lucky Plaza, 315 -321 Lockhart Rd Wan Chai, Hong Kong (hereafter referred to as “ Seeed ”); Unless the context requires otherwise , Customer and Seeed shall hereinafter collectively referred to as the “Parties” or individually referred to as the “Party ”.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Parties', which is explicitly defined in the 'Contradicted Text' section as Customer and Seeed. The changed text replaces 'Parties' with 'Personnel'. The Perturbation Explanation says that it's unclear if “personnel” is equal the “Recipient” leading to uncertainty of who should pay in violation. This creates a contradiction because the original agreement clearly defines who the 'Parties' are (and therefore who is responsible), while the changed text introduces ambiguity by using 'Personnel', potentially conflicting with the original definition and creating uncertainty about liability.

perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise agreed in writing this Agreement shall gov ern Confidential Information disclosed by the Parties after the effective date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise agreed in writing this Agreement shall gov ern Proprietary Information disclosed by the Parties after the effective date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: Here, "Confidential Information" is replaced with "Proprietary Information". This is a major contradiction because proprietary Information is not defined anywhere, while "Confidential Information" has a whole section dedicated to the definition, making enforcement confusing.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, “Confidential Information” shall mean the fo llowing: i. All records, files, analysis, documents, software, computer or electronic data disks or tapes, test data, printouts, processes, designs, file layout, technical bulletins, manuals, diagrams, formulas, research, inventions, patents and discoveries reasonably related to the Parties’ businesses or products and services of the Parties that have not been publicly released; ii. Technical, financial, business plan and customer information, including standard periodic financial statements and analyses, budget s, tax returns, benefit and compensation plans, customer list(s) and contact names, functional and technical specifications; iii. Other valuable information disclosed by one of the Parties to another, designated as confidential expressly or by the circumstance s in which it is provided.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Confidential Information" to "Proprietary Information" creates a contradiction because "Confidential Information" is specifically defined in the contradicted text, while "Proprietary Information" is not defined anywhere in the document. This misalignment of terminology, as described in the Perturbation Explanation, creates an in-text contradiction, because the contract now uses a term that has no definition leading to conflicting obligations and creating uncertainty about what is covered by the agreement. This matches the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
